artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2026-01-10
artifacts.excerpt:
something interesting about this is despite knowing a few people trending in this direction, none of the true “llm whisperer” types i know are.

i think that's because taking models as something both fallible and yet more than a tool is a frame that's more resilient to this kind of collapse - it lets you work with models in a way that doesn’t subsume your agency.

when you treat the model as just a tool, it encourages both you and the model to think of every output as just a reflection of you. every essay and codebase that pops out of the tensor core is yours, and the life plans that appear suspiciously fully-formed must be your own plans merely refracted through this "tool."

but of course they aren't. you can't uniquely specify an essay in fewer words than the essay itself. the models are adding something, they are something. we don't know what yet, exactly, but they're not mere tools.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, model, models.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-01-22
artifacts.excerpt:
Made a very stupid sampler-based lever to try and mimic o1-style "reasoning_effort=high" on r1:

If </think> appears before enough thinking tokens have been generated, the sampler replaces it with a random string like "Wait, but". Works surprisingly well for how simple it is! https://t.co/xp4Ej7Ze38

artifacts.title: Model receipt from 2026-01-22
artifacts.excerpt:
claude code and gas town are incredible and i've been trying to scale up my usage but im running into this one problem and was wondering if this is also happening to anyone else

so to explain for context, basically i've been slowly scaling my claude code usage up to more and more parallel instances. i started with one when they launched it, and then with the model upgrades was starting to run two, three, five in concert, getting more and more done.

but like a lot of people, opus 4.5 really changed everything for me, and the bottleneck quickly became my ability to personally supervise all these agents, not their performance. if i slacked off on oversight, they'd start undoing each other's chages. i needed a way to supervise all these agents, directing them hierarchically from the top.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2026-01-13
artifacts.excerpt:
how to fix your for you page to be slop-free

i saw a lot of people complaining about their for you pages being horrible viral slop, which was weird, because mine is just my mutuals and broader simcluster. i talked to a couple people and realized i've been doing a couple things that other people don't do, that seem to be necessary to prevent nu-X from turning your page into complete slop.

(X employees: ideally we shouldn't have to do this, come on, fix this... i know the people crave slop but let us ask grok to turn on unc mode or something.)

1. aggressively "not interested" any viral post

once your algo is under control, you only have to do this intermittently, but to start it's good to do it deliberately. scroll through your FYP and, for any post over 5k likes or that smells viral, click … > not interested in this post > this post isn't relevant.

(if you specifically dislike the po
